# Revis Studio

> Revis Studio is a custom carpet atelier in New York City, founded in 2001 by Toby Revis. The studio designs heirloom hand-knotted Tibetan rugs from Himalayan long-staple wool, drawn in Manhattan and woven by master craftspeople in Kathmandu, Nepal. Revis serves architects, interior designers, fine hotels, luxury retail, and private residential clients worldwide.

## Quick facts

- **Founded:** 2001
- **Founder & creative director:** Toby Revis (former certified gemologist at Van Cleef & Arpels; senior buyer for the Elsa Peretti collection at Tiffany & Co.; Syracuse University, University of Madrid, New York School of Interior Design)
- **Atelier:** 225 East 74th Street, #6N, New York, NY 10021, USA (by appointment)
- **Phone:** +1 212 987 8544 (studio) · +1 917 293 0707 (mobile)
- **Email:** toby@revisstudio.com
- **Instagram:** @revisstudio
- **Speciality:** Bespoke hand-knotted Tibetan rugs, custom carpets, designer trade resource
- **Materials:** Tibetan long-staple Himalayan wool, Chinese mulberry silk, Himalayan nettle (allo); vegetable dyes (madder, indigo, walnut, pomegranate) or premium Swiss synthetic dyes
- **Construction:** Traditional Tibetan cut-loop knot, hand-spun yarn, batch dyed, hand washed in spring water, sun-stretched
- **Typical knot density:** 100 knots per square inch (denser available on request)
- **Typical lead time:** 12 – 26 weeks from brief to installation
- **Sizes:** From 2 × 14 ft runners to 20 × 30 ft seamless fields; round, oval, irregular, stepped and inset shapes
- **Service model:** Direct-to-source — one studio, one point of contact, no intermediaries

## Core pages

- [Home](https://revisstudio.com/): Overview of the studio, signature projects, process and material.
- [Studio](https://revisstudio.com/studio.html): The story behind Revis Studio and a short biography of founder Toby Revis.
- [Craft](https://revisstudio.com/craft.html): A detailed guide to materials (Tibetan wool, silk, allo), dye (vegetable and Swiss), the Tibetan hand-knot, finishing and a glossary of carpet terms.
- [Projects](https://revisstudio.com/projects.html): Selected commissions — Manhattan duplexes, Tribeca lofts, Watermill retreats, East Hampton retail, Sonoma residences, Catskills homes.
- [Press](https://revisstudio.com/press.html): Editorial features in Architectural Digest, Luxe Interiors + Design, Sonoma Magazine, Elle Decor, 1stDibs Introspective and The New York Times.
- [Journal](https://revisstudio.com/journal.html): Field notes on dye, scale, light, and what makes a custom rug worth commissioning.
- [Contact](https://revisstudio.com/contact.html): Inquiry form, studio address and direct contact for Toby.

## Services

- **Custom carpet commission:** A complete bespoke service from concept and color study through hand-knotting and on-site installation. For private residential, hospitality and retail clients.
- **Trade programme:** Direct-to-source pricing and a single point of contact for interior designers, architects and decorators. Color and yarn consultation, full-size sampling, project management.

## What designers say

- "Toby's carpets are works of art and we would not be at our best without her beautiful rugs and impeccable service." — Deborah Hancock, Partner, Rees Roberts + Partners LLC
- "If you can dream it, Revis Studio can weave it. Her 'above and beyond' service makes Revis Studio a treasured resource for designers, architects and decorators." — Amy Lau, Principal, Amy Lau Design
- "It is always a pleasure doing business with Toby. She does not hesitate to go the extra mile, and her attention to detail is second to none." — Sara Story, Principal, Sara Story Design
- "In a field laced with delays, production problems and inconsistent quality, I have come to rely on her for her broad knowledge and color sense." — Lisa Frazer, Architect, Studio Frazer

## Frequently asked questions

- **How long does a Revis custom rug take?** Twelve to twenty-six weeks from first conversation to installation. A 9 × 12 carpet at 100 knots per square inch typically requires six to nine months on the loom; design, sampling and finishing add the remainder.
- **What materials are used?** Tibetan long-staple Himalayan wool (base fibre, prized for its high lanolin content), hand-reeled Chinese mulberry silk and Himalayan nettle (allo) for accent highlights. Dyes are vegetable pigments (madder, indigo, walnut, pomegranate) or premium Swiss synthetic dyes when UV stability is critical.
- **Do you work with interior designers?** Yes. Revis Studio is built around the design trade and offers direct-to-source pricing, sampling and a single point of contact (Toby) from sketch to installation. Trade enquiries to toby@revisstudio.com.
- **Where is the studio?** 225 East 74th Street, #6N, New York, NY 10021. Visits by appointment, Monday through Friday.
- **What sizes can be produced?** Runners as narrow as 2 × 14 ft, seamless fields as large as 20 × 30 ft. Round, oval, irregular, stepped and inset shapes are produced from room dimensions.
- **How are Revis rugs different from showroom designer rugs?** Revis is the maker, not a reseller. No warehoused inventory, no minimum reorder run. Each carpet is drawn, dyed and knotted for one room.
